sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Arch Capital Group stock hits 52-week low at 19.52 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Arch Capital Group Ltd's ADR stock has dropped to a 52-week low of $19.52, reflecting a 5.32% decline over the past year. Despite this, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued and oversold, trading at a P/E ratio of 8.22 with a high dividend yield of 6.91%. Investors are closely watching the insurance company's performance amidst broader financial market trends.

feed.title: Walmart Digital Price Tags Coming to All U.S. Stores in 2026
feed.summary: Walmart plans to install digital price tags in all its U.S. stores by the end of 2026, a transition that began two years ago. While Walmart states this aims to simplify operations and unify pricing, customers express concerns about dynamic pricing and issues with the tags themselves. The company denies plans for dynamic pricing for customers and assures that price updates are human-led and maintain their "Everyday Low Price" promise.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Introduces Glacios 3 Cryo-TEM for Structural Biology Labs
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched the Glacios 3 Cryo-TEM, a new 200 kV cryogenic transmission electron microscope designed to make advanced structural biology tools more accessible to laboratories. This instrument integrates AI-powered software and a flexible installation system, the READY System, to enhance throughput, data quality, and ease of use while reducing facility constraints. The Glacios 3 Cryo-TEM aims to empower more institutions and researchers to leverage cryo-EM for complex biological questions and therapeutic development.

feed.title: [Form 3] Ads-Tec Energy Public Ltd Co Initial Statement of Beneficial Ownership
feed.summary: Ads-Tec Energy Public Ltd Co director Andreas Fabritius has filed an initial statement of beneficial ownership (Form 3), disclosing a direct holding of 22,257 ordinary shares. These shares include restricted stock units (RSUs) granted under the company's 2021 Omnibus Incentive Plan, some of which are already vested and others will vest in the future. The filing details the vesting schedule of these RSUs.

feed.title: A $6B AI buildout could lift Corning jobs 15%-20% in North Carolina
feed.summary: Corning Incorporated and Meta Platforms, Inc. have broken ground on a significant expansion of Corning's optical cable manufacturing capacity in Hickory, North Carolina. This expansion supports a multiyear, up to $6 billion agreement where Corning will supply Meta with advanced optical fiber, cable, and connectivity solutions for AI data centers. The project is expected to increase Corning's employment in North Carolina by 15% to 20% and reinforces the state's role as a manufacturing hub for optical technology.

feed.title: AMTD owned Global Art Media - The Art Newspaper Officially Registered in Hong Kong with the Government and Launches Its Inaugural Hong Kong Edition
authors: AMTD Digital; AMTD IDEA Group; The Generation Essentials Group
feed.summary: AMTD Group, AMTD IDEA Group, AMTD Digital Inc., and The Generation Essentials Group announced that "The Art Newspaper" has been officially registered in Hong Kong and launched its inaugural Hong Kong edition during Art Basel Hong Kong 2026. This follows AMTD's 2023 acquisition of The Art Newspaper and the expiration of the previous licensing agreement. The registration also paves the way for the upcoming launch of The Art Newspaper China in October, both produced and published by AMTD directly.

feed.title: Golden Pass LNG starts producing after QatarEnergy and Exxon's Middle East facilities shut down
feed.summary: The Golden Pass LNG project, a joint venture between Exxon Mobil Corp. and QatarEnergy in Sabine Pass, Texas, has commenced production from its first LNG train. This development follows previous contracting challenges and shutdowns at other facilities owned by the companies in the Middle East. QatarEnergy holds a 70% stake in Golden Pass LNG, while Exxon owns the remaining 30%.

feed.title: International Paper plans $225m Mississippi plant
feed.summary: International Paper announced plans to invest $225 million in a new 468,000-square-foot corrugated packaging facility in Brandon, Mississippi. This new plant will replace an older facility nearby, aiming to modernize its manufacturing footprint, improve efficiency, and strengthen service capabilities in the Mid-South region. Construction is expected to start in June with operations by the fourth quarter of 2027.

feed.title: F5 BIG-IP APM systems vulnerable to critical remote code execution flaw | brief | SC Media
feed.summary: A severe security flaw in F5's BIG-IP APM systems, initially underestimated as a denial-of-service issue, has been reclassified as a critical remote code execution (RCE) threat with a severity score of 9.8 out of 10. The vulnerability, CVE-2025-53521, allows attackers to gain complete control of affected servers without authentication by planting malware. F5 has released indicators of compromise, urging customers to patch immediately or rebuild systems if a compromise is suspected.

feed.title: Blackstone Dumps Spanish Rental Portfolio In $1.4 Billion Brookfield Deal
feed.summary: Blackstone has agreed to sell its entire Fidere residential rental portfolio in Spain to Brookfield Asset Management Inc. for a gross 1.2 billion euros ($1.4 billion). This transaction, valued at a net 1.05 billion euros, includes approximately 5,000 units across 47 buildings in Madrid, marking the largest multifamily deal in Spain since the 2007–2009 financial crisis, excluding Blackstone's acquisition of Testa Homes. Additionally, Blackstone recently participated in a consortium acquiring the Royal Challengers Bengaluru cricket franchise for an estimated $1.78 billion, aligning with its strategy of investing in high-growth sports franchises.

feed.title: AI Startup “Upstage” in Talks to Purchase 10,000 Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Chips in Korea
feed.summary: South Korean AI startup Upstage is in discussions with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to acquire 10,000 MI355 AI accelerators, aiming to enhance its domestic compute capacity and diversify from Nvidia. This move supports Upstage's participation in a government-backed contest to develop national AI foundation models. Meanwhile, AMD anticipates first-quarter revenue of approximately $9.8 billion, including $100 million from China-related AI chip sales.

feed.title: Broadcom Inc Stock (AVGO) Moved Up by 4.58% on Mar 31: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) saw its stock rise by 4.58% due to strong Q1 FY26 earnings, exceeding expectations with robust AI revenue guidance. The company launched new AI infrastructure and cybersecurity products and secured a significant government contract, leading to positive analyst upgrades. Despite some technical sell signals and low media coverage, Broadcom is positioned favorably within the AI-driven semiconductor industry, though it faces risks like insider selling and supply bottlenecks.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc Class A Stock (GOOGL) Moved Up by 4.23% on Mar 31: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. Class A stock (GOOGL) rose by 4.23% on March 31, driven by the announcement of "TurboQuant," an AI memory-compression algorithm, and the acquisition of cloud and AI security platform Wiz. Analysts have reiterated "Buy" ratings with increased price targets, signaling confidence despite ongoing antitrust scrutiny and geopolitical threats. Technical indicators, however, suggest caution with sell signals from MACD, RSI, and Williams %R.

feed.title: Is NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) one of the Best Goldman Sachs AI Stocks: Top 12 Stocks to Buy?
feed.summary: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) is highlighted as a top Goldman Sachs AI stock, actively innovating in the AI computing space. The company recently launched a Language Processing Unit, unveiled new rack systems, and introduced NemoClaw, a business AI platform, indicating significant advancements. CEO Jensen Huang projects substantial revenue from new chips by 2027, emphasizing the company's commitment to meeting the escalating demands of agentic AI workloads despite limited recent stock movement.

feed.title: Basin Electric, NextEra Energy hold open house to discuss data center
feed.summary: Basin Electric and NextEra Energy hosted an open house in Stanton, ND, to address community concerns regarding a proposed hyper-scale data center and gas power plant. The projects, which would require significant land and create 280-535 permanent jobs, face community skepticism despite company assurances. Residents expressed frustration over a lack of clear answers and called for more transparency and continued discussions.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) CEO gets equity awards and tax share withholding
feed.summary: Mark W. Kowlzan, Chairman & CEO of Packaging Corp of America (PKG), received 43,010 shares from TSR performance units and 3,292 shares from accumulated dividend equivalents, with performance units paying out at 200%. To cover withholding taxes, 20,513 shares were withheld by the company at $212.25 per share. After these transactions, Kowlzan directly holds 482,876 shares of common stock, along with indirect holdings in a 401(k) plan and shares held by his spouse (beneficial ownership disclaimed).

feed.title: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (NYSE:DD) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours (NYSE:DD) experienced a 14.6% decrease in short interest during March, reducing the total to 7,417,252 shares. The company surpassed quarterly EPS estimates and provided strong FY2026 guidance, leading several analysts to raise price targets, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating. Institutional investors have also shown increased confidence, with many modifying their holdings.

feed.title: Tesla (TESLA) and SpaceX Plan to Build a New Chip Factory in Texas
feed.summary: Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX are planning to build two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas, as part of a "Terafab" project. This initiative aims to address future chip demand for Tesla's vehicles and Optimus robots, as well as AI data centers in space, given that current global supply only meets a small fraction of their projected needs. The Terafab venture is a collaboration involving SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI, with no completion date announced.

feed.title: Why Aehr Test Systems Stock Jumped Today
feed.summary: Aehr Test Systems (NASDAQ: AEHR) saw its stock jump 21.3% after announcing it secured a major new customer, an undisclosed global networking leader. This new customer is developing advanced silicon photonics-based transceivers for data center networking, aligning Aehr with the surging demand for AI processor equipment. The company expects additional orders and sees this as a multi-year expansion opportunity driven by the growth of fiber optic interconnects in hyperscale AI data centers.

feed.title: 10 Information Technology Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies 10 Information Technology stocks that have triggered "whale alerts" based on unusual options activity detected by Benzinga's scanner. It provides a table and bullet-by-bullet explanations of specific call and put option trades for companies such as AppLovin, Broadcom, Oracle, and Shopify, including details like strike price, expiration date, trade sentiment, and total trade price. The purpose of these alerts is to help traders discover potential opportunities arising from significant institutional options transactions.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Cuts Vornado Realty Trust (NYSE:VNO) Price Target to $28.00
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Vornado Realty Trust (NYSE:VNO) from $32.00 to $28.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This adjustment implies a potential upside of 7.68% from the current stock price. Other analysts have also trimmed their targets, resulting in a consensus "Hold" rating and a price target of $35.55 for the company.

feed.title: [144] GLOBE LIFE INC. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article details a Form 144 SEC filing by GLOBE LIFE INC. (GL) on March 31, 2026. The filing indicates a notice of proposed sale of 12,000 shares of common stock resulting from a stock option exercise, with an aggregate market value of $1,637,400. The shares are to be sold through APEX CLEARING on the NYSE.

feed.title: Tesla named on Iran’s 18-company target list threatening Gulf operations
feed.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has included Tesla on a list of 18 US companies whose Middle East operations are threatened with attack in retaliation for military leader killings, setting an explicit deadline of April 1. Tesla's significant physical presence in the Gulf, including showrooms and Supercharger stations, makes it a unique target compared to other tech and defense companies primarily operating data centers. The threat comes as Tesla has been actively managing regional instability, including offering free Supercharging, and its inclusion on the list is notable given its lack of defense contracts.

feed.title: Had You Invested $1,000 in Micron or Western Digital 10 Years Ago, Here’s How Much You’d Have Today
feed.summary: A $1,000 investment in Micron Technology ten years ago is now worth approximately $29,919, outperforming the S&P 500 significantly due to its transformation into a critical AI infrastructure supplier. While Western Digital also saw substantial gains, particularly from its Sandisk spinoff and AI-fueled demand, Micron is presented as the cleaner bet on AI memory infrastructure due to its more reasonable valuation and stronger position as the only U.S.-based high-bandwidth memory manufacturer. The article highlights the differing paths and market positions of these two memory and storage giants in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts, Limited (NASDAQ:WYNN) Sees Large Growth in Short Interest
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ: WYNN) experienced a significant 20% increase in short interest in March, reaching 6,833,602 shares, representing about 6.6% of its stock. This rise suggests increased bearish sentiment towards the casino operator, with a days-to-cover ratio of 2.7 days based on average trading volume. Despite missing analyst EPS estimates in its last quarterly report, the company's revenue slightly exceeded expectations, and analysts maintain a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $138.87.

feed.title: VSE Corporation (NASDAQ:VSEC) Sees Significant Increase in Short Interest
feed.summary: VSE Corporation (NASDAQ:VSEC) experienced an 18.3% increase in short interest during March, reaching 2,253,292 shares, which represents about 8.2% of its shares sold short. Despite this bearish sentiment, institutional ownership is high at 91.54%, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $233.71. The company also reported strong Q1 earnings, beating estimates with EPS of $1.16 and revenue of $301.2 million, and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.10.

feed.title: Genuine Parts Company announces date for Q1 results
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) has announced that it will release its first-quarter financial results on April 21, 2026. Following the release, the company will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET, which will be accessible via webcast on their investor relations website. A replay of the call will also be available on the company's website.

feed.title: Uber confirms holding a 5.8% passive stake in WeRide
feed.summary: This article is severely truncated and only mentions that Uber holds a 5.8% passive stake in WeRide. No further details are provided.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley raises Apellis stock price target to $41 on Biogen deal
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley raised its price target for Apellis Pharmaceuticals to $41 from $25, maintaining an Equalweight rating, following Biogen's definitive agreement to acquire Apellis. The acquisition, valued at approximately $5.6 billion in upfront cash plus a contingent value right, represents an 86% premium to Apellis's 90-day volume-weighted average price. Biogen anticipates the deal will enhance its revenue growth and be accretive to its non-GAAP diluted earnings per share starting in 2027.

feed.title: Lawsuit Filed Against Aldeyra Therapeutics Over Clinical Trial Inconsistencies
feed.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: ALDX) alleging the company failed to disclose inconsistent results from its reproxalap clinical trials, leading to unreliable positive findings. This non-disclosure reportedly caused a significant drop in Aldeyra's stock price, harming investors. The lawsuit, filed by Gainey McKenna & Egleston, highlights the importance of transparency in pharmaceutical clinical trials and the legal risks of withholding material information from shareholders.

feed.title: Harvard’s Top Board Adds Blackstone CFO, Ex-Cabinet Secretary
feed.summary: Harvard University has appointed Michael Chae, Blackstone Inc.'s CFO, and Sylvia Mathews Burwell, former Secretary of Health and Human Services, to its governing board. These appointments come as the university faces increased pressure. Burwell brings government and higher education experience, while Chae contributes financial expertise and is already involved with Harvard's endowment management.

feed.title: Leith Wheeler Canadian Equity Reduces Stake in Bank of Montreal by 28.74%
feed.summary: Leith Wheeler Canadian Equity significantly reduced its stake in Bank of Montreal (TSX:BMO) by 28.74% in the fourth quarter of 2025, impacting its portfolio by -1.12%. This move was part of broader strategic adjustments, which also included adding Keyera Corp (TSX:KEY) and Rockpoint Gas Storage Inc (TSX:RGSI) to its portfolio, and increasing positions in stocks like Constellation Software Inc (TSX:CSU) and Intact Financial Corp (TSX:IFC). The fund's investment strategy focuses on long-term returns from a diversified portfolio of Canadian equities, driven by fundamental research and a value bias.

feed.title: McCormick to Acquire Unilever Foods in $44.8B Deal
feed.summary: McCormick & Company, Inc. will acquire Unilever's foods business in a $44.8 billion deal, combining brands like McCormick, Knorr, and Hellmann's into a global portfolio with $20 billion in revenues. Unilever will receive a mix of McCormick stock (65% equity, $29.1 billion) and $15.7 billion in cash, allowing it to focus solely on beauty, wellbeing, personal care, and home care sectors. The transaction, expected to close by mid-2027, aims for $600 million in annual cost synergies and establishes Brendan Foley as the combined company's chairman, president, and CEO.

feed.title: McDonald’s to launch new $3 value menu, $4 meal this week
feed.summary: McDonald's is introducing a new value menu in April, featuring items priced at $3 or less, such as sausage biscuits and 4-piece Chicken McNuggets. The fast-food giant will also offer a $4 breakfast meal that includes a McMuffin, hash brown, and coffee. These initiatives aim to address customer frustration over pricing and reestablish McDonald's as an affordable dining option.

feed.title: Hilton Releases Q1 2026 Earnings Report Date
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (NYSE: HLT) announced it will release its first quarter 2026 financial results before the market opens on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. A conference call with President & CEO Christopher J. Nassetta and EVP & CFO Kevin Jacobs will follow at 9 a.m. EDT to discuss the results and answer questions. A live webcast will be available on the Hilton Investor Relations website, with a replay and transcript available within 24 hours.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc Stock (AMZN) Moved Up by 3.63% on Mar 31: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc (AMZN) saw a 3.63% stock increase on March 31, driven by its expanded partnership between AWS and OpenAI, strong advertising business performance, and advancements in AI and quantum computing. Despite this positive movement, significant intraday volatility reflects investor caution due to Amazon's substantial capital expenditure plans for 2026 (estimated at $200 billion) and broader macroeconomic uncertainties like inflation and geopolitical tensions. Analysts are divided, with some maintaining "Buy" ratings while others express reservations regarding the impact on free cash flow and increasing debt levels.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group Launches Industry-First Tri-Branded Credit Cards
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has partnered with Bank of America to launch the Royal ONE™ Visa Signature® and Royal ONE Plus™ Visa Signature® credit cards, which are the cruise industry's first tri-branded cards. These cards allow travelers to earn and redeem rewards across Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea, enhancing the loyalty experience and offering accelerated rewards and travel perks. This initiative aims to provide more flexibility and value for guests across the company's vacation brands.

feed.title: From fundraising to first response: How Cintas’ commitment to heart health saved a life
feed.summary: Cintas employees supported the American Heart Association (AHA) by raising over $180,000 for the Cincinnati Heart Mini-Marathon & Walk. The article highlights how Cintas's commitment to CPR training directly led to employee Dan Gray saving his neighbor's life through quick action. Cintas was recognized as the 2026 AHA Torch Trophy winner for its exceptional fundraising and dedication to heart health preparedness.

feed.title: Transylvania U launches financial literacy initiative with WesBanco
feed.summary: Transylvania University has partnered with WesBanco to launch a new digital financial literacy initiative. This program offers online courses, mentorship, workshops, and classroom engagement covering essential financial topics for students, faculty, and staff. The collaboration aims to equip participants with the knowledge and confidence to make informed financial decisions and enhance real-world readiness.

feed.title: Technology: Stocks Give Back Some 2025 Strength During the First Quarter
feed.summary: The technology sector experienced a significant rebound in 2025 after bottoming in April but saw a downturn in the first quarter of 2026, becoming the third-weakest sector as software industry weakness outweighed semiconductor and hardware strength. Despite this, Morningstar maintains confidence in secular tailwinds like cloud computing, AI, and semiconductor demand, highlighting software as having the most upside. The report identifies Broadcom, Microsoft, and NXP Semiconductors as top picks, emphasizing their opportunities in AI and other growth areas.

feed.title: Alkermes (ALKS) Shares Surge by Nearly 18% in Market Rally
feed.summary: Alkermes (ALKS) shares surged by 17.7%, reflecting strong investor interest and positive market momentum for the biotechnology company which focuses on drug manufacturing. The company demonstrates robust financial health with strong profitability margins and a solid balance sheet, including a low debt-to-equity ratio and high institutional ownership. While the valuation metrics show a mixed picture, analyst sentiment is positive with a "Buy" consensus, though investors should watch for insider selling activity and sector-specific risks.

feed.title: NRG Energy (NRG) Analyst Ratings Update: Barclays Lowers Price Target
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained its "Overweight" rating for NRG Energy (NRG) but adjusted its price target from $203.00 to $200.00, a decrease of 1.48%. This update comes from analyst Nicholas Campanella and follows several other recent analyst rating changes for NRG Energy. The company, a major retail energy provider and independent power producer in the US, has an average target price of $194.42 from 15 analysts, suggesting a 35.17% upside from its current price, though GuruFocus estimates a 19.4% downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Blackstone Sets New Benchmark With Record Life Sciences Fund
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. announced the final closure of its Blackstone Life Sciences VI fund (BXLS), securing a record $6.3 billion in capital commitments, making it the largest private fund dedicated to life sciences. The fund was oversubscribed and surpassed its predecessor by nearly 40%. Blackstone Life Sciences boasts a strong track record, including an 86% success rate for Phase III assets, and has recently committed to significant investments, including a $700 million R&D funding agreement with Merck.

feed.title: American Airlines Joins Delta Air, United, Southwest, Alaska, JetBlue and More US Aviation Companies Stocks Soaring High, But Tourism Sector Sees a Fragmented Profit: New Update is Here
feed.summary: US aviation company stocks, including American Airlines, Delta Air, United, Southwest, Alaska, and JetBlue, are experiencing a powerful surge driven by strong demand and strategic shifts. However, the broader tourism sector is seeing fragmented profits due to uneven demand and changing traveler behavior, indicating that growth is selective and execution-dependent rather than uniform. Investors are now evaluating airlines on individual strategic models, such as Delta's premium strength or JetBlue's turnaround efforts, making company-specific fundamentals crucial for stock performance.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: American Shared Hospital Services misses Q4 2025 EPS forecast
feed.summary: American Shared Hospital Services (AMS) reported a significant miss for its Q4 2025 earnings, with an EPS of -$0.09 against a forecast of $0.04 and revenue of $7.7 million, down 14.8% year-over-year. The company faces liquidity concerns due to a 68% decrease in cash and challenges with financial covenants, though management is actively engaging with lenders. Despite operational challenges and falling stock prices, the company is strategically shifting towards direct patient care services and expanding its footprint with new partnerships and facilities, anticipating future growth.

feed.title: Akanda (NASDAQ: AKAN) adds 200km Mexico fiber, locks in $2M cash
feed.summary: Akanda Corp., through its subsidiary First Towers & Fiber Corp. (FTF), has expanded its dark fiber network in Central Mexico by approximately 200 kilometers, bringing total coverage to about 900 kilometers. This expansion is supported by a long-term Indefeasible Right of Use agreement expected to generate approximately $2.0 million in contracted cash flow over 10 years from existing enterprise customers. The company views this as the initial step in a broader strategy to consolidate premium fiber assets in Mexico, especially within the Bajío industrial corridor, to build a scalable digital infrastructure platform with high margins.

feed.title: WaFd Inc (WAFD) Stock Price Quote Today & Current Price Chart
feed.summary: This article provides current stock price quotes and a price chart for WaFd Inc (WAFD). It also mentions online trading with a smart investment app from Capital.com.

feed.title: Eaton to establish data center-focused segment
feed.summary: Eaton is establishing a new business segment dedicated to the booming data center market, following its recent $9.5 billion acquisition of Boyd Corp.'s thermal business. Angie McMillin will lead this new global data center segment, which aims to provide cohesive solutions, particularly in liquid cooling, to address the increasing power consumption and heat generation from data centers and AI technologies. This move reflects Eaton's strategic response to growing demand and includes plans for increased production capacity in the U.S.

feed.title: Ermenegildo Zegna N.V. (NYSE:ZGN) Short Interest Down 14.2% in March
feed.summary: Ermenegildo Zegna N.V. (NYSE:ZGN) experienced a 14.2% decrease in short interest in March, with total short interest amounting to 3,214,792 shares by March 13th. This represents 0.8% of outstanding shares and a short-interest ratio of 4.4 days. Despite missing recent quarterly earnings expectations, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $11.73, following an upgrade from UBS.

feed.title: AIRO Reports Q4 2025 Results, Introduces 15–25% 2026 Growth Outlook and $150M Backlog
feed.summary: AIRO Group Holdings announced its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, reporting revenue of $48.3 million for Q4 and $90.9 million for the full year. The company introduced a 2026 revenue growth outlook of 15% to 25% year-over-year and revealed a consolidated backlog of approximately $150 million expected to convert over the next 12 months. Key operational highlights include the completion of the first U.S.-manufactured RQ-35 Heidrun ISR drones and strategic partnerships for advanced unmanned systems development.
